The Senate reconciliation bill uses a 'current policy' baseline to extend the 2017 TCJA individual tax cuts. This approach allows for the extension of tax cuts without counting their cost under current budget rules. President Trump signed a reconciliation bill on July 4, 2025, that includes these extensions.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, modifies several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Production Tax Credits (45Y). The OBBBA introduces accelerated repeal schedules, shortens eligibility deadlines, tightens domestic content rules, and imposes new foreign entity of concern (FEOC) restrictions. Specifically, the OBBBA harmonizes the domestic content requirements under Section 48E with those under Section 45Y for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025, making domestic content requirements mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y).

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act, signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Investment Tax Credits (48E). The Act imposes specific domestic-content percentages for Section 48E projects, making these requirements mandatory for obtaining the credits. For example, it sets a domestic content percentage of 40% for facilities starting construction before June 16, 2025, and increases it to 45% for facilities starting construction after June 16, 2025, but before January 1, 2026. This change indicates that domestic content compliance will become mandatory for full Investment Tax Credit eligibility in 2025.

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) and introduces new requirements for the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credits (45X). The bill imposes Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions, including requirements for domestically-produced materials or subcomponents, and prohibits credits for taxpayers identified as specified foreign entities or foreign-influenced entities. The legislation advances to the Senate, where it may be amended before final enactment.

Paul Biya has been officially declared the winner of the 2025 Cameroonian presidential election, securing 53.7% of the vote. This confirms his eighth term as President of Cameroon. The Constitutional Council announced the results, and Biya has been sworn in for another seven-year term.

The President's Malaria Initiative (PMI) faces an uncertain future due to the Trump administration's foreign aid review and reorganization. The new 'America First' Global Health Strategy emphasizes bilateral agreements and reduced funding for non-commodity and non-frontline health personnel. These changes suggest that PMI could cease to exist as a distinct organization before January 1, 2026.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBB) was signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025. This law introduces significant changes to the 45X clean energy manufacturing tax credit, including capping credits. The law imposes new restrictions on the ability of projects to generate tax credits if certain Foreign Entities of Concern (FEOCs) are involved. These changes will occur before January 1, 2026.

Based on the evidence provided, India has already surpassed Japan to become the world's fourth-largest economy. With a reported GDP of about $4.18 trillion, India is expected to continue growing at a faster pace than Germany.
